sections.paragraph:
ἀποσκίασμα, ατος, τό (ἀποσκιάζω ‘cast a shadow’; Aëtius [100 a.d.] 2, 30, 3: Dox. Gr. 361b, 21. ἀποσκιασμοί Plut., Pericl. 155 [6, 5]) shadow τροπῆς ἀ. a shadow cast by variation (in position of heavenly bodies) Js 1:17 (Theopomp. [?]: 115 Fgm. 400 Jac. τὸ ἀποσκίασμα τῆς τοῦ ἡλίου ἀνταυγείας). JRopes, ICC ad loc. and Goodsp., Probs. 189f prefer the rdg. of B et al. (παρ.) ἡ τροπῆς ἀποσκιάσματος ‘(no variation) of changing shadow’. MDibelius, Meyer7 1921 ad loc. would emend to put both nouns in the genitive and transl. the clause ‘who is without change and knows neither turning nor darkness.’—DELG s.v. σκιά. M-M. TW.

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Theopomp Theopomp , hist., IV b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

ICC ICC = International Critical Commentary—List 6

Goodsp., Probs. Goodsp., Probs. = Problems of NT Translation—List 6

rdg. rdg. = reading(s)

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

παρ.̠par. = parallel

Meyer Meyer = EM., Ursprung und Anfänge des Christentums—List 6

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

transl. transl. = translate, translation

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 120
